LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRUNSWICK CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. AS PLAINTIFF APTLY NOTES, MOTIONS TO STRIKE ARE DISFAVORED AND THEIR USE IS TO "CAUTIOUS AND SPARING" - NOT AS A "PROCEDURAL LINE ITEM VETO." (PH II, INC. V. SUP. CT. (1995) 33 CAL.APP.4TH 1680, 1683.) DEFENDANT ARGUES THAT A NOVATION SUPERSEDED PRIOR CONTRACTS BETWEEN THE PARTIES, BUT THIS RAISES SEVERAL FACTUAL AND CONTRACT-INTERPRETATION ISSUES NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO RESOLUTION AT THE PLEADING STAGE. THE SAME IS TRUE OF DEFENDANT'S DISPUTES WITH THE DAMAGE THEORIES PLED. THE FACTS PLAINTIFF PLEADS IN ITS FRAUD CLAIM WOULD SUPPORT PUNITIVE DAMAGES.ORDER IS SIGNED. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRUNSWICK CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE PLiNTIF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's amended complaint is DENIED. As plaintiff aptly notes, motions to strike are disfavored and their use is to "cautious and sparing" - not as a "procedural line item veto." (PH II, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683.) Defendant argues that a novation superseded prior contracts between the parties, but this raises several factual and contract-interpretation issues not susceptible to resolution at the pleading stage. The same is true of defendant's disputes with the damage theories pled. The facts plaintiff pleads in its fraud claim would support punitive damages.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
